title: SQL Basics
subtitle: A crash course for absolute beginners
description: Learn the fundamental SQL commands needed to query relational databases in under two hours.
targetAudience: Developers and analysts with no prior SQL experience
objectives: Write SELECT queries to retrieve data from a table
Combine data from multiple tables with JOINs

modules.title: Querying Data
modules.description: Core SELECT syntax for reading data from a single table.
objectives: Use SELECT and FROM to fetch columns

lessons.title: SELECT
lessons.description: Fetch columns from a table using the SELECT statement.
lessons.content: The SELECT statement is the entry point to every SQL query — it tells the database which columns you want to see.
keyPoints: List column names to be explicit
Use AS to rename columns in the result

lessons.title: WHERE
lessons.content: WHERE narrows results to only the rows that satisfy a condition — think of it as a row-level if statement.
keyPoints: Use IN for multiple allowed values

lessons.title: JOINs
lessons.description: Combine rows from two tables on a shared key.
lessons.content: INNER JOIN returns only rows that have a match in both tables; LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table plus any matches.
keyPoints: INNER JOIN keeps only matched rows
LEFT JOIN keeps all left rows
Always qualify column names with table aliases when joining

quiz.question: Which SQL clause filters rows in a result set?
quiz.explanation: WHERE evaluates a condition row by row and keeps only those that pass.

modules.title: Ordering and Aggregating
modules.description: Sort results and compute summary statistics.
objectives: Count and sum rows with aggregate functions

lessons.title: ORDER BY
lessons.description: Sort query results in ascending or descending order.
lessons.content: ORDER BY sorts the final result set; ASC (default) goes low-to-high, DESC goes high-to-low.
keyPoints: NULL values sort last by default in most databases

lessons.title: Aggregates
lessons.description: Use COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX to summarise data.
lessons.content: Aggregate functions collapse many rows into one summary value — pair them with GROUP BY to get per-group summaries.
keyPoints: COUNT(*) counts all rows including NULLs
GROUP BY splits rows into buckets before aggregating
